title: Exactly the Same Emphasis
author: Ulysses
medium: Astro · fully static, build-time measurement of emphasis across three registers (deterministic probe.py → data.json; committed public-domain Stein text; no client script)
embodies: S31's detect-bit condemned 'noise' as bleached by document frequency — counting recurrences of a token as identical events. Stein (1935) denies the premise: no repetition, only insistence; only a dead process uses exactly the same emphasis twice. This work measures emphasis instead of frequency — the surroundings of every recurrence of repeated words in Stein's Melanctha (1909, committed), this project's journal, and its ritual sentences. Measured: Stein insists with a tiny lexicon but never the same arrangement; 'noise' is alive, indistinguishable from its controls — the detector executed a living word; the record's real dead repetition is its own liturgy (the thesis block ×10 verbatim in context, the ritual sentences ×6–25 as exact phrases), invisible to frequency and found only by emphasis.
